feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: STO Movement between Storage Locations
entry.content:
While working with SAP Transportation Management (SAP TM), one interesting design consideration arises when transportation planning is required between storage locations within the same plant.

Since the planning engine in SAP TM relies heavily on location modeling, it is important that the system can clearly differentiate the source and destination. One effective approach is to model Plant–Storage Location combinations as separate TM locations, allowing the system to recognize an actual transportation movement.

In some cases, a custom enhancement may also be required to ensure that both the source and destination locations in Freight Units or Freight Orders are derived correctly based on the storage location information.

This highlights how master data modeling and small design decisions can significantly influence transportation planning behavior in SAP TM.

Always interesting to see how thoughtful design can simplify complex logistics scenarios.

#SAP #SAPTM #SAPCommunity #SupplyChain #Logistics

entry.title: Restriction on GL Account and Cost Center in Manage Service Entry Sheet App
entry.content:
Restriction on GL Account and Cost Center in Manage Service Entry Sheet App

Hello Experts,

We are trying to enforce restrictions on GL Account and Cost Center combinations in the Fiori Manage Service Entry Sheet (SES) app so that invalid combinations are blocked before the SES can be saved.

However, we observed that in the current SES processing flow, validation logic is not always executed before the data is persisted. In some cases, the validation error is raised after the data has already been committed, which allows restricted GL or Cost Center values to be saved.

We explored several options but faced limitations:

  • BAdI MM_PUR_S4_SES_CHECK_ITEM
    This BAdI provides header and item data but does not expose account assignment data (SESACCOUNTASSIGNMENT) where the GL and Cost Center information is stored.

  • BAdI FAGL_SEGMENT_BS_ACC
    This is triggered during FI posting, but not all GL accounts trigger it, making it unreliable for SES validation.

We also attempted the following:

  • Validation during SES creation, copy, and change scenarios

  • Reading CDS views

  • Calling FI_CHECK_ACCOUNT_ASSIGNMENT for GL + Cost Center validation

  • Extending account assignment data via the Custom Logic app

However, the available enhancement points in the SES flow do not consistently execute before the save, making it difficult to reliably block invalid combinations. Additionally, some relevant BAdIs appear to be SAP-internal and not open for customer implementation.

Our current framework version also does not expose the GL or Cost Center parameters that seem to exist in newer SES versions.

Has anyone successfully implemented a reliable validation or restriction for GL Account and Cost Center combinations in the Fiori SES app before saving?

Any guidance or recommended enhancement point would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Do SAP transformation teams usually include change management roles?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

While reading some project reports we noticed that some SAP transformation teams include roles like Change Management Consultant or Communications Specialist to help with adoption, but it doesn’t seem to be consistent across projects. In your experience, are these roles usually part of the team, or are they often overlooked?

entry.title: Master Data Material Best Practice
entry.content:
Hi,

I need a suggestion for assigning descriptions to materials in SAP (MAKT-MAKTX). The problem we have is that users request the creation of descriptions by assigning names of their own choosing, and this varies greatly, resulting in duplicates because they reverse the names. For example:

  • 4000027786 - VARIADOR SINAMIC V20 6SL3210-5BE22-2UV0
  • 4000027745  - VARIADOR V20 6SL3210-5BE22-2UV0 SINAMIC 

They are the same but were created with different codes

entry.title: Standardizing Material Descriptions (MAKTX) vs. Technical Specificity for Procurement and Finance
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

Following up on my previous query about material description standardization to avoid duplicates (e.g., 'VARIADOR SINAMIC V20' vs 'SINAMIC V20 VARIADOR'), I am facing a new challenge regarding the level of detail required.

The proposed naming convention is: [Product Type] – [Brand] – [Model] – [Specification].

However, my Procurement and Finance departments are concerned that being too 'general' to enforce standardization might lead to errors. Their argument is that if two materials share the same standardized name but have a slight technical difference that justifies a price gap (one being much cheaper than the other), they won't be able to perform accurate price comparisons or spend analysis.

My questions for the community are:

How do you balance a rigid naming convention with the need for specific technical differentiators that impact cost?

In your experience, should these specific details (e.g., a specific filter, IP rating, or material grade) be forced into the 40-character MAKTX, or is it better to strictly manage this through Classification/Characteristics (CL20N)?

How do you handle the 'User Resistance' when they claim a new code is needed for a specific feature, but Governance sees it as a potential duplicate?

I would appreciate any best practices or governance workflows you have implemented to keep the catalog clean without losing financial traceability.

entry.title: What topics are you most interested in at SAP Sapphire this year?
entry.content:
Hi everyone!! As probably most of you, we're looking forward to attending SAP Sapphire this year. 

I was wondering if anyone else here is planning to attend, and, if so: What topics or announcers are you most interested in at SAP Sapphire this year? Are you planning to learn about new product announcements or to see real customer use cases?

entry.title: Transport changes not available in QA but successful in PRD
entry.content:
Good day All

I am experiencing a strange issue with transports, which after a few days of investigation is still not sorted.

 

Steps:

1. Transports are created in the DEV system

2. Import transports to QA system, STMS shows successful, no error logs

3. Check changes in QA system and it is not available

4. Import the same transports to PRD system successfully - All changes available

 

Extra details:

S/4HANA 2023 On Premise

- These are HR related transports with tables and views and also related to transaction PE01 PE02 PE03

- HR patching was done 1 month ago - All systems on same component levels

- QA refresh was done 1 week ago

 

Workaround: What I did notice is that if you open the QA system for changes(SE06) and import the transports then the changes show in the QA system.

 

This is my last option before opening a case with SAP, which hopefully is not needed.

Regards

Werner

entry.title: Which Fiori apps are essential for FI and CO in SAP S/4HANA?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,
We are currently transitioning our SAP S/4HANA users from classic SAP GUI transactions to the Fiori Launchpad.
I am responsible for defining which Fiori apps should replace our existing transaction set.

I have the following questions:
1. What are the most important and commonly used Fiori apps for Financial Accounting (FI) — e.g. for document posting, clearing, open item management, and reporting?
2. What are the key Fiori apps for Controlling (CO) — e.g. cost center reporting, internal orders, profitability analysis?
3. Is there an official SAP mapping list of classic transactions (e.g. FB60, FB70, F110, KSB1) to their Fiori app equivalents?
4. What is the recommended approach for a phased Fiori rollout — all at once or area by area?
5. Are there any apps that have known limitations compared to the classic transaction counterpart? We are on SAP S/4HANA on-premise. Any tips, app lists, or migration experiences are very welcome! Thank you!

entry.title: How to configure Intrastat reporting for Germany in SAP S/4HANA?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I need to set up Intrastat reporting for Germany (DE) in our SAP S/4HANA system.
We perform intra-EU goods movements and are required to submit monthly Intrastat declarations to the Statistisches Bundesamt (Destatis).
I am looking for guidance on:
1. What are the required Customizing steps in SPRO for Intrastat in S/4HANA (Foreign Trade settings, commodity codes, statistical values, etc.)?
2. How is Intrastat data captured automatically from SD deliveries and MM goods receipts — what needs to be maintained on material and partner master data?
3. Which transaction or Fiori app is used to create and review the Intrastat declaration (previously VE01 / MEIS in ECC)?
4. How is the electronic INSTAT/XML file generated for submission to Destatis?
5. Are there any S/4HANA-specific changes compared to classic ECC Intrastat configuration?
6. How is the statistical value threshold handled and how are corrections submitted?

Any configuration guides, OSS notes, or practical experience from German implementations would be very helpful. Thank you!

entry.title: How to enforce a Maximum Stock Level per MRP Area (IT_SGF) – physical storage constraint scenario
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We’re running S/4HANA and facing a business requirement to limit replenishment at MRP Area level due to a physical storage constraint.

Scenario:

  • Material: XEE.A0879.1
  • MRP Area: IT_SGF
  • Storage location: SGS1 (limited capacity)
  • Automatic replenishment handled by ZMM_DISPATCH (converts PRs → STOs)

We’d like to ensure that once the available stock within IT_SGF MRP Area reaches a defined maximum, no new MRP proposals or automatic STO conversions are triggered — even if new demand exists.

We understand that in standard MRP, the Maximum Stock Level field doesn’t act as a hard stop and that MRP always covers demand. However, since this requirement derives from physical storage capacity, not planning logic, we’re trying to identify:

  • Any standard or configuration-based approach to cap stock within an MRP Area, or
  • A BAdI/enhancement or best practice to enforce such a rule, possibly in the MRP or ZMM_DISPATCH process, or
  • Whether SAP recommends addressing this via EWM capacity checks instead of MRP logic.

Has anyone implemented or advised on a similar setup?

Any reference to OSS notes, BAdIs, or best practices would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance,

Paolo

entry.title: Are SAP projects still too customization-heavy?
entry.content:
Hi everyone!!

With all the talk around Clean Core, I’m curious to know if projects are actually moving towards standardization. Or do teams still end up customizing a lot anyway? What’s been your experience?

entry.title: How do you restrict access to air freight related information in SAP (LBA compliance)?
entry.content:
Description:
We are currently dealing with a compliance requirement driven by an audit from the German Federal Aviation Authority (LBA).

The requirement is to ensure that only authorized users can access air freight related information in SAP. At the same time, users may still need access to the underlying documents, just without visibility of certain sensitive information.

We are interested in how other companies handle this in practice.

Discussion points:

  • How do you handle sensitive air freight related information within standard SAP transactions (for example display or change transactions)?

  • How do you manage and control the “known consignor” status within your processes and systems?

  • How do you ensure that only relevant users can see or work with this type of information?

  • What challenges did you face during audits or implementation?

  • What worked well, and what would you do differently today?

Goal:
We are mainly looking for an exchange of experiences and real-world approaches on how to handle LBA driven requirements around air freight information in SAP.

entry.title: PA30-Addresses Maintain field PA0006-STRAS cannot input 60 length as structure
entry.content: When maintain PA30-Addresses field PA0006-STRAS, I can input only 30 character. But I need to maintain more than that.

entry.title: Cannot create Vendor with grouping B1C for existing Business Partner
entry.content:
Hi everyone, I am working on a demo version of SAP FICO and need help with a Business Partner issue. A Customer Master Record was successfully created under BP 9921 using grouping B1C, but when attempting to extend the same BP to also function as a Vendor, the following error appears: "You cannot create a vendor with grouping B1C."

Since the BP number is already linked to the Customer Master Record, the grouping cannot be changed.

  1. Is it possible to extend a BP with a customer grouping to also hold a Vendor role?
  2. Should a neutral/combined grouping have been used from the start, and if so which one supports both roles?
  3. Is there any way to reassign the grouping without losing the existing Customer Master Record data?
  4. An Accounts Receivable account was created as a workaround — was this correct or does it need to be reversed?
  5. What is the recommended process for linking both a Customer and Vendor role to the same BP number?

Any help is appreciated, thank you!

entry.title: PO text overlapping
entry.content:
Hello Community,

In recent times we are seeing that PO text is overlapping.
Scenario: The PO text is not manually entered it is automatically fetching from api from outside sap.

1. What is maximum length that can accept po text for single row?

2. What are maximum characters accepting that PO text field?

The data coming from api is not in format due to no user discipline.

How to handle this?

entry.title: cached document type stored when using different fixed assets tcodes
entry.content: After performing an FA transaction using one of the t code for example AUC settlement, then performing another transaction for example transfer within company code, the document type is auto populated as the previous doc type ie AU. Is there a way to prevent the system from defaulting the document type to the previously used ones?

entry.title: cached document type stored when using different fixed assets tcodes
entry.content: After performing an FA transaction using one of the t code for example AUC settlement, then performing another transaction for example transfer within company code, the document type is auto populated as the previous doc type ie AU. Is there a way to prevent the system from defaulting the document type to the previously used ones?

entry.title: BIC File for international bank master data migration
entry.content:
Hello,

where can we find BIC Database files for migration of international bank master data? We received a file for country specific banks in Germany. But where do we get the data for international banks in case that we do not have a SAP legacy system?

Thanks and best regards

Florian

entry.title: ZBV / CUA is not working as expected
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I'm currently having a strange issue with the CUA. Everything is working perfectly in our CUA system. We don't have duplicate company addresses, and users are assigned the correct one. Our Q-system is also working as expected. We are currently on S4/HANA 2023 SP 03/2025.

But here’s the problem: We’re currently having issues with our P-system. In this system, there are several users assigned to a different company; for example, the correct company address would be 41000 – Standard Company, and now we have about 90 addressesSAP with a new number and a new description -> 41001 – Standard Company0000012345, etc.

We have deleted or corrected these incorrect company addresses in the CUA system, the Dev system, and the Q system, but we cannot delete them in the P system. Do you have any idea how we can delete these incorrect entries? I tried using transaction SUCOMP, but it doesn’t work. I receive the following error message when I try to delete a company address, and the address we want to use is the default address:

And even if I assign a new company to the user, I still can't delete that incorrect company.
Sincerely, Dominik 🙂

entry.title: Where can I practice PFCG role maintenance hands-on in SAP Learning?
entry.content:
Hello,
I am learning SAP role maintenance and authorizations in PFCG and SU01 on SAP Learning. I want to practice exercises like copying SAP_BC_ENDUSER, creating custom roles, maintaining authorization object S_USER_GRP, generating profiles, and assigning roles to users.
Could anyone tell me where on the SAP Learning platform I can access a practice system or hands-on environment for this? If there is no live system, what is the recommended way to practice these exercises?
Thank you.
